name: Ley Line Walker
description: A spell-casting wizard so attuned to ley lines that he can see magic energy (P.P.E.) and draw on ley-line power as a living relay and P.P.E. battery. Prefers lightweight weapons and armor, since full body armor and bionics disrupt the flow of magic.

concealedBodyArmor.notes: Seldom wears heavy/full body armor; conventional environmental armor prevents spell casting unless made mostly of natural M.D.C. materials.

ppe.notes: Permanent Base P.P.E. = 3D6x10+20 added to the character's P.E. attribute number. Plus 3D6 P.P.E. per additional level starting at level two.

perLevelUp.note: One new spell of level up to the character's own experience level (not one from each level).

spellKnowledge.notes: See 'The Pursuit of Magic' (p.185). Additional spells/rituals of any level can be learned or purchased at any time. Larger spell selection in Rifts Book of Magic.

abilities.name: Sense Ley Line and Magic Energy
abilities.summary: Automatic sensing powers, no P.P.E. cost.

senses.name: Sense a Rift
senses.range: within 50 miles (80 km); +10 miles per level starting at level two

senses.name: Sense Magic in Use
senses.range: 100 feet (30.5 m) per level; not psionics

senses.name: See Magic Energy
senses.detail: Sees P.P.E. aura when >20 P.P.E. present; can see the magically invisible. Costs one melee action while willed in place. Range line of sight ~1000 ft (305 m). Does not work on Invisibility: Superior.

abilities.name: Read Ley Lines
abilities.summary: Automatic. Instantly learns a ley line's direction, length, nexus points, open Rifts, and major natural disasters along it. No P.P.E. cost.

abilities.name: Ley Line Transmission
abilities.summary: Send a verbal/visual message along a ley line to anyone on the line (one-way unless recipient is also a mage with the Transmission spell). Automatic, no P.P.E. 01-20% chance a Telepath senses it.

abilities.name: Ley Line Phasing (teleportation)
abilities.summary: Teleport flawlessly anywhere on the same ley line. Requires 1D4 melees of concentration. Automatic, no P.P.E.

usesPer24h.examples: 6 at L1, 8 at L2, 10 at L3

abilities.limits: Only self + possessions; must stay on the same ley line (switch lines only at a nexus).

abilities.name: Ley Line Walking / Line Drifting
abilities.summary: Walk or float along a ley line at Speed 10, no exertion, no P.P.E. Can meditate while drifting. Height typically 1-5 ft, up to the height of the line.

abilities.name: Ley Line Rejuvenation
abilities.summary: On a ley line, doubles natural healing. Instant rejuvenation once per 24h: fully rested and heals 20 H.P. + 20 S.D.C., +1D6 H.P. and 2D6 S.D.C. (or 4D6 M.D.C. if a Mega-Damage being) per level. No P.P.E. Restores H.P./S.D.C. only, not P.P.E./I.S.P.

abilities.name: Ley Line Observation Ball
abilities.summary: Conjure a linked scrying sphere on a ley line.

abilities.name: Affinity with Rift & Ley Line Magic
abilities.summary: These dimensional spells are most commonly known by Ley Line Walkers but the LLW does NOT start with any at level 1 (only the Ley Line Rifter does). Learned over time: one per new experience level via a 48-hour meditative trance.

abilities.name: Ley Line Force Field
abilities.summary: On a ley line, summon an Armor of Ithan-like field.

stats.note: Regenerates at full strength next melee if destroyed; draws 20 ambient ley-line P.P.E. per melee while up.

abilities.name: Initial Spell Knowledge
abilities.summary: Master of spell magic (tends to avoid ritual magic but can perform rituals). At level 1, select any three spells from each of magic Levels 1-4 (12 total). See spellKnowledge.

abilities.name: Learning New Spells
abilities.summary: Additional spells and rituals of any level can be learned and/or purchased at any time regardless of experience level. See 'The Pursuit of Magic'.

abilities.name: P.P.E.
abilities.summary: Greatest permanent P.P.E. of all mortal practitioners of magic. See ppe.

categoryRules.allowed: First Aid or Paramedic (Paramedic counts as two skills)

secondarySkills.notes: From the Secondary Skills list; no category bonuses (only a high-I.Q. bonus may apply). Start at base skill level.

bonuses.detail: +1D4 to any one Mental attribute (I.Q., M.E., or M.A.)

bonuses.note: The number others must save against when you cast a spell.
Double when on a ley line.

standardEquipment: 100 ft (30.5 m) of lightweight cord and grappling hook
weapons: Survival knife, hand axe, automatic pistol or submachine-gun (player's choice), energy pistol or rifle, and several extra clips of ammunition.
vehicle: Usually a Techno-Wizardry device, hover vehicle, motorcycle, jet pack, or Wing Board — inexpensive and easy to replace, since they often leave vehicles behind when phasing.
cybernetics: Starts with none and avoids augmentation because it interferes with magic. Bio-System prosthetics considered only if necessary.
